items_en: 1. Takes note of the report of the Governing Council of the United Nations Human Settlements Programme (UN-Habitat) on its nineteenth session 9 and the report of the Secretary-General on the special session of the General Assembly for an overall review and appraisal of the implementation of the outcome of the United Nations Conference on Human Settlements (Habitat II) and the strengthening of UN-Habitat 10;

2. Recognizes the primary responsibility of Governments for the effective and effective implementation of the Habitat Agenda 1 and the Declaration on Cities and Other Human Settlements in the New Millennium,2 and stresses the need for the international community to fulfil fully its commitments to assist Governments in developing countries and countries with economies in transition by providing them with the necessary resources, capacity-building, technology transfer and an enabling international environment;

3. Encourages Governments to include issues related to housing, sustainable human settlements development and urban poverty in their national development strategies, including poverty reduction strategy papers, if any;

4. Urges Governments to promote investment in services and facilities, in particular in pro-poor water and sanitation facilities, in order to improve living conditions, especially in slums and informal settlements;

5. Encourages Governments to establish urban observatories at the local, national and regional levels and to provide financial and technical support to UN-Habitat for the development of new methods of data collection, analysis and dissemination;
6. Also encourages Governments to support and facilitate the participation of youth in the implementation of the Habitat Agenda through the

items_en: 7. Encourages Governments and UN-Habitat to continue to promote partnerships with local authorities, non-governmental organizations, the private sector and other Habitat Agenda partners, including women's groups, as well as academic and professional groups, with a view to enabling them, in accordance with the legal framework and taking into account country-specific conditions, to contribute more effectively to the creation of adequate shelter for all and the sustainable development of human settlements in an urbanizing world;

8. Encourages UN-Habitat to continue to cooperate closely with other relevant United Nations agencies, including members and observers of the United Nations Development Group and members of the Inter-Agency Standing Committee; 11

9. Requests UN-Habitat to intensify its efforts to make the Cities Alliance an effective means of achieving the dual objective of the Habitat Agenda: adequate shelter for all and sustainable human settlements development in an urbanizing world;

10. Welcomes the efforts made within the framework of the partnership between the World Bank and UN-Habitat, the Cities Alliance, as well as other donor countries, to continue to provide a privileged framework for policy development and coordination and to provide support for the development of pro-poor urban planning strategies and slum upgrading programmes, in accordance with the legal framework and taking into account country-specific conditions;

11. Invites the Secretary-General to include in his report on the review of the implementation of the Millennium Declaration in 2005 an assessment of the progress made towards the goal of achieving a significant improvement in the living conditions of at least 100 million slum-dwellers by 2020;

12. Welcomes the efforts of the Executive Director of UN-Habitat to mobilize funds, resulting in an increase in general-purpose contributions to the United Nations Habitat and Human Settlements Foundation for 2003;

13. Calls for continued financial support for UN-Habitat through increased voluntary contributions to the Foundation, and invites Governments to provide multi-year funding to support programme implementation;

14. Requests UN-Habitat to collaborate with the Division for Sustainable Development of the Department of Economic and Social Affairs of the Secretariat in the preparations for the twelfth session of the Commission on Sustainable Development, with a view to generating fruitful discussions on the thematic cluster on water, sanitation and human settlements issues;
15. Requests the Executive Director of UN-Habitat to inform the Governing Council of the United Nations Human Settlements Programme

items_en: 16. Notes that the forthcoming sessions of the World Urban Forum, a non-legislative technical body, to be held in Barcelona in 2004 and Vancouver in 2006, will provide an opportunity for experts to exchange experiences, as well as information on best practices and lessons learned in the field of human settlements;
17. Invites donor countries to assist representatives of developing countries to participate in the second and subsequent sessions of the World Urban Forum;

18. Requests the Secretary-General to continue to review the resource requirements of UN-Habitat and the United Nations Office at Nairobi so that UN-Habitat and other United Nations bodies and agencies in Nairobi can be effectively served;

19. Requests UN-Habitat, as the focal point for sustainable human settlements development issues and for the coordination of activities in this regard within the United Nations system, to work towards the centralization of human settlements issues in order to contribute to the overall coordination of humanitarian efforts, including by participating in the consideration by the Economic and Social Council, in the near future, of the issue of the transition from relief to development;
20. Requests the Secretary-General to submit to the General Assembly at its fifty-ninth session a report on the implementation of the present resolution;

21. Decides to include in the provisional agenda of its fifty-ninth session an item entitled "Special session of the General Assembly for an overall review and appraisal of the implementation of the outcome of the United Nations Conference on Human Settlements (Habitat II) and strengthening of the United Nations Human Settlements Programme (UN-Habitat)".
